def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    atr_window = params['atr_window']
    breakout_multiplier = params['breakout_multiplier']
    rsi = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=rsi_window).rsi()
    atr = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_window).average_true_range()
    upper_band = df['close'] + breakout_multiplier * atr
    lower_band = df['close'] - breakout_multiplier * atr
    long_signal = (df['close'] > upper_band) & (rsi > 50)
    short_signal = (df['close'] < lower_band) & (rsi < 50)
    signals = pd.Series(np.where(long_signal, 1, np.where(short_signal, -1, 0)), index=df.index)
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/GBP exhibits momentum breakouts where price tends to trend strongly after breaching volatility-adjusted bands confirmed by RSI momentum.
